---
species: "Laminaria digitata"
accepted_code: "LADI"
alternate_codes: "LD" 
diminutive: "LADIJ" 
alternate_diminutives: "LDJ" 
common_name: "Fingered Kelp"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
description: "Hand-like kelp. juvenile <10cm" # make sure to fill this in!
image: '/header_images/laminaria_digitata_sarahbeecy.jpg'
image_credit: 'Photo taken by Sarah Beecy in Small Point, ME.'
categories:
  - brown algae
  - kelp
sampling_programs:
    - keen_quad
    - keen_swath
    - keen_upc
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_rov
    - bhi_bruv
    - bhi_farms
    - lsw_quad
    - lsw_microquad
habitat:
  - intertidal
  - subtidal
mobility:
  - sessile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 80300902
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 145724
---

**Other images:** <!-- Other images with attribution -->
<br/>

**Distinguishing features:**  A large, brown, canopy forming kelp. Can be found intertidally or subtidally. Cleanly divided blade with finger-like sections. Typically reaches a length of 2-3 meters.
<!-- size, gross morphology, a key identifier, zonation notes (intertidal or depth) -->
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** Can be confused for *Saccharina latissima*, which does not have finger-like sections.

<br/>

**Extended Description:** <!-- Extended description -->

**[WORMS page](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=taxdetails&id=145724)**
